desc: Krong Kep is a quiet Cambodian seaside town known for crab-market seafood, low-key beaches, and access to pepper farms and Kep National Park trails.
hls: Kep crab market and Kampot pepper pairing
Compact town suited to slow 2-4 night stays
Easy links to Kampot and Rabbit Island

todo.name: Crab market lunch
todo.desc: Pick fresh blue crab dishes with Kampot pepper sauces.

todo.name: Kep National Park loop trail
todo.desc: Do the shaded ridge loop early for cooler temperatures.

todo.name: Rabbit Island boat day
todo.desc: Take a small boat for beach downtime and simple seafood shacks.

todo.name: Kampot pepper farm visit
todo.desc: Tour a certified pepper plantation outside town.

todo.name: Colonial villa hill drive
todo.desc: Explore remaining French-era villas around the hillside roads.

todo.name: Sunset at sailing club
todo.desc: End the day with sea views and low-key cocktails.

wifi.note: Adequate in most guesthouses; backup mobile data recommended.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland access is typically one-stop to Phnom Penh (often via Bangkok, Singapore, Doha, or Kuala Lumpur) followed by road transfer; winter dry-season demand raises room rates.
prac.currency: Cambodia local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Many beach roads have limited lighting—avoid late scooter trips if unfamiliar with local routes.
Cash (USD and riel) remains important despite growing QR adoption.
Heavy rains can disrupt island boats in June-September afternoons.

sweet: Dec-Mar for drier weather and calmer coastal conditions.
